delegation.delegation_quote_verbatim: 이 정도는 그냥 너가 판단해도 되는 수준
delegation.delegation_scope: routine same-class judgments delegated to the orchestrator; the named human additionally set the C4 approach directly (allow line re-stacking; a minimal sibling-label nudge is permitted as label-position vocabulary; never commit a known real collision; final C4 development verdict routes back through the delegation).

finding.summary: The 2 remaining open accidental frame-crossing labels from C3 (Panel E 'grounded substrate'; Panel F 'grounded source return'), now attempted with line re-stacking allowed.

panelE_grounded_substrate.detail: Restacked 'grounded substrate' to two lines 'grounded'/'substrate' (established in-figure convention; no wording/font change). The 2-line stack (0.360 cm tall) did not fit the 0.373 cm vertical band between the sibling 'sample' label and the apparatus-box bottom border, so 'sample' was nudged up 0.06 cm (2.72 -> 2.78) into its own 0.30 cm of headroom below 'motion stage', opening the band to 0.433 cm. This is a two-label position edit, strictly label-position vocabulary, explicitly authorized. Result: both Panel E frame crossings removed with zero new findings.

panelF_grounded_source_return.detail: The ground-symbol referent sits at panel-x [3.89,4.15], only 0.60 cm from the right frame (4.75). Even the narrowest sensible restack line ('grounded', 0.713 cm) cannot clear the frame (needs right edge <=4.65 => left edge <=3.937) without covering the referent (x>=3.89); shifting up to clear the referent detaches the 0.056 cm leader stub and clips the HV wire. Label-only position/anchor/restack vocabulary is geometrically exhausted for this label. Its fix belongs to a future authoring/layout iteration (widen Panel F, or move the ground symbol/HV inward), not a bounded label repair.

authorized: restack 'grounded substrate' to two lines and nudge the sibling 'sample' label the minimal amount to open the vertical band
record 'grounded source return' as a permanently-open declared defect
not_authorized: final C4 development verdict is issued by the orchestrator under delegation (recorded separately)
any non-label element move, panel/frame resize, wording or font change
